description: Remove the naive grocery combine pipeline (~500 lines), delegate all combining to the AI edge function (returns both combined and per-recipe items in one call), remove unused cook mode, unify the duplicated Add Recipe/Add Meal dialogs into a shared RecipeInputForm component, delete dead code, and achieve 100% test coverage.

userStories.title: Delete naive combine pipeline from groceryList.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the combineIngredients() function and all its supporting constants/helpers from groceryList.ts so that the codebase is simplified and we eliminate the source of bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: combineIngredients() function is deleted

All unit conversion constants are deleted: VOLUME_IN_TSP, WEIGHT_IN_OZ, DENSITY_OZ_PER_CUP, COUNT_TO_CUP, COUNT_TO_LB, CAN_TO_CUP, SLICE_TO_COUNT, FLUID_OZ_INGREDIENTS, BULK_CONVERSIONS, PREFERRED_COUNT_UNIT, UNIT_REMAP
ADJECTIVES_TO_STRIP and PRESERVED_COMPOUNDS are deleted
INGREDIENT_ALIASES constant is deleted
normalizeIngredientName() function is deleted
normalizeUnit() function is deleted
UNIT_MAP constant is deleted
filterPantryItems() (non-smart version) is deleted
ALWAYS_PLURAL and NO_STRIP_S constants are deleted (only used by normalizeIngredientName)

These functions/constants are kept: GROCERY_CATEGORIES, CATEGORY_ORDER, CATEGORY_OVERRIDES, decimalToFraction, parseFractionToDecimal, formatGroceryItem, UNIT_PLURAL_MAP, NAME_FIRST_UNITS, ABBREVIATION_UNITS, groupByCategory, filterSmartPantryItems, generateCSV, generatePlainText, downloadCSV, smartCombineIngredients, SmartCombineResult, detectCategory, FRACTION_MAP
detectCategory() simplified to use name.toLowerCase().trim() instead of normalizeIngredientName()
filterSmartPantryItems() simplified to use item.name.toLowerCase().trim() instead of normalizeIngredientName()
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: This is a large deletion. combineIngredients is defined starting around line 476 and runs ~275 lines. The supporting constants (UNIT_MAP, INGREDIENT_ALIASES, ADJECTIVES_TO_STRIP, PRESERVED_COMPOUNDS, ALWAYS_PLURAL, NO_STRIP_S, normalizeIngredientName, normalizeUnit, all the conversion maps) span lines 36-475. filterPantryItems is at line 848. Keep CATEGORY_OVERRIDES (line 171) — it's used by detectCategory() which IngredientFormRows depends on. GroceryListSection.tsx imports combineIngredients, filterPantryItems, normalizeIngredientName, normalizeUnit — these imports must be removed (handled in US-004). Tests will break — those are fixed in US-006.

userStories.title: Simplify GroceryListSection to use AI results for all tabs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want GroceryListSection to render AI-provided items for both the combined tab and per-recipe tabs, removing all naive combine logic and the displayNameMap concept.
acceptanceCriteria: combineIngredients, filterPantryItems, normalizeIngredientName, normalizeUnit imports are all removed
All naive combine variables are removed: combinedItems, filteredItems, excludedItems, pantryExcludedCount, groupedItems
The naive fallback rendering block (!smartGrouped && !combineError) is removed
New prop added: perRecipeItems?: Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]> (AI-provided per-recipe combined items)
displayNameMap prop is removed — no longer needed
Per-recipe tabs render from perRecipeItems[recipe.name] instead of constructing items from raw RecipeIngredient[]
Per-recipe tabs apply pantry filtering via filterSmartPantryItems on their items
Combined tab renders from smartGroceryItems (unchanged)
Error state (combineError) still shows error message on combined tab
The pantry excluded items section is removed or reworked to use smart items only
recipeIngredients prop is still used for hasAnyIngredients check and ingredientsByRecipe (for tab visibility)
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: GroceryListSection.tsx is 405 lines. The key change: per-recipe tabs no longer construct SmartGroceryItem[] from raw RecipeIngredient[] with displayNameMap lookups. Instead, they receive fully-formed SmartGroceryItem[] from the AI via perRecipeItems prop. This means combined and per-recipe tabs use the exact same quality of AI output. The recipeIngredients prop is still needed to determine which recipes have been parsed (for tab visibility) and hasAnyIngredients. The groupSmartByCategory helper at the top of the file (lines 32-43) is still used for both tabs. Remove the displayNameMap prop from GroceryListSectionProps. Add perRecipeItems?: Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]> prop. For per-recipe tab rendering (lines 278-310), replace the manual mapping logic with: const recipeItems = perRecipeItems?.[recipe.name] ?? []; then filter and group as usual.

userStories.title: Update SmartCombineResult and smartCombineIngredients for unified AI response
userStories.description: As a developer, I want smartCombineIngredients() to send raw ingredients to the AI and receive both combined and per-recipe items in one call, eliminating the displayNameMap concept.
acceptanceCriteria: SmartCombineResult type changed from { items, displayNameMap } to { items: SmartGroceryItem[], perRecipeItems: Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]> }
smartCombineIngredients() no longer calls combineIngredients()
smartCombineIngredients() maps raw RecipeIngredient[] to format: { name, quantity, unit, category, recipeName }
The mapped ingredients are sent to the edge function as 'rawIngredients' (not 'preCombined')
perRecipeNames parameter is removed — no longer needed since AI returns perRecipeItems keyed by recipe name
Return type uses the updated SmartCombineResult
Function still throws on error or when AI returns skipped
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: smartCombineIngredients is at src/lib/groceryList.ts around line 864. Currently it calls combineIngredients() at line 870, maps to preCombined format, and passes perRecipeNames. The new version: (1) map RecipeIngredient[] directly to { name, quantity, unit, category, recipeName } using recipeNameMap, (2) send as rawIngredients to edge function, (3) receive { items, perRecipeItems } back. The perRecipeNames parameter is no longer needed because the AI figures out per-recipe grouping from the recipeName field on each ingredient. The edge function is updated in US-007.

userStories.title: Rewrite edge function for unified combined + per-recipe response
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the combine-ingredients edge function to accept raw ingredients and return both combined items and per-recipe items in one response, so that all tabs get the same quality AI output.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge function accepts 'rawIngredients' array with shape: { name, quantity, unit, category, recipeName }
Edge function no longer accepts 'perRecipeNames' — per-recipe grouping is derived from recipeName field
System prompt instructs AI to: (1) combine all ingredients across recipes into 'items', (2) combine ingredients within each recipe into 'perRecipeItems' keyed by recipe name
System prompt handles ALL combining: contextual unit handling (keep 19oz for cans), deduplication, name normalization, alias resolution (scallion=green onion), categorization, displayName generation
System prompt includes rules about keeping different products separate (sesame oil != vegetable oil, dried oregano != fresh oregano)
System prompt includes contextual unit conversion rules (don't convert can sizes, do convert tsp/tbsp)
Response format: { success, items: SmartGroceryItem[], perRecipeItems: Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]> }
displayName on each item is quantity-aware (pluralized correctly for that item's quantity context)
Dropped-ingredients validation adapted for raw input (check unique ingredient names, not individual entries)
Edge function tests updated for new input/output format
npm run test:coverage passes with 100% on edge function tests
userStories.notes: Edge function is at supabase/functions/combine-ingredients/index.ts (222 lines). Major rewrite needed. The PreCombinedInput interface changes to RawIngredientInput with recipeName field. The system prompt needs to explain two outputs: 'items' (combined across all recipes) and 'perRecipeItems' (combined within each recipe, keyed by recipe name). A single recipe can have duplicate ingredients (e.g., '1 cup flour' for cake + '2 tbsp flour' for dusting) — the AI must combine these in perRecipeItems. Both items and perRecipeItems entries get proper displayName with quantity-aware pluralization. Keep the must-merge rules, displayName rules, and contextual unit conversion rules from the current prompt. The validation at lines 178-195 needs to handle duplicates in raw input (multiple recipes may have 'garlic'). Edge function test file: tests/unit/edge-functions/combine-ingredients.test.ts.

Remove tests that rely on combineIngredients or naive fallback. Per-recipe tab tests should now pass perRecipeItems prop (Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]>) and verify items render correctly. Remove all displayNameMap prop references. The combineError prop should show an error message. All SmartGroceryItem fixtures must include the required displayName field.

userStories.title: Update cache, migration, and remaining tests for 100% coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to update the grocery cache to store perRecipeItems instead of displayNameMap, add a migration, and achieve 100% test coverage across all required directories.
acceptanceCriteria: groceryCache.ts: GroceryCacheResult uses perRecipeItems?: Record<string, SmartGroceryItem[]> instead of displayNameMap
groceryCache.ts: saveGroceryCache accepts perRecipeItems instead of displayNameMap as 6th param
groceryCache.ts: loadGroceryCache returns perRecipeItems instead of displayNameMap
groceryCache.ts: stores data in per_recipe_items column (via cast, same pattern as display_name_map)
New migration added: ALTER TABLE combined_grocery_items ADD COLUMN per_recipe_items jsonb DEFAULT '{}' (display_name_map column can remain for backward compat)
groceryCache.test.ts: updated for perRecipeItems instead of displayNameMap
groceryEdits.test.ts: fixtures use SmartGroceryItem with displayName
EventDetailPage.test.tsx: mocks return SmartCombineResult with perRecipeItems, test perRecipeItems passed to GroceryListSection
MealPlanPage.test.tsx: same updates as EventDetailPage tests

npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
No remaining references to displayNameMap in codebase
No remaining references to deleted functions/types (combineIngredients, CombinedGroceryItem, normalizeIngredientName, etc.)
All 3 recipe entry flows verified: URL parsing, file/photo upload, manual entry — no imports of deleted functions
userStories.notes: groceryCache.ts currently stores display_name_map. Change to per_recipe_items. Use same cast pattern (column not in generated Supabase types). Add migration at supabase/migrations/ with next timestamp. Keep display_name_map column in DB for backward compat (old cached rows) — loadGroceryCache should handle rows that have display_name_map but not per_recipe_items gracefully (return undefined for perRecipeItems). Required 100% directories: src/components/events/, src/components/ingredients/, src/components/mealplan/, src/components/recipes/, src/lib/. Exception: IngredientWheel.tsx (~55% acceptable). IMPORTANT: Verify all 3 recipe entry paths are unaffected: (1) URL parsing — parse-recipe edge function with url, (2) File/photo upload — PhotoUpload.tsx + parse-recipe with file content, (3) Manual entry — AddMealDialog, IngredientFormRows, EditRecipeIngredientsDialog. These produce RecipeIngredient[] and don't depend on removed functions. EditRecipeIngredientsDialog imports parseFractionToDecimal (kept) and deleteGroceryCache (kept). IngredientFormRows imports detectCategory (kept, simplified). Run final: npm run test:coverage && npm run build && npm run lint.

userStories.title: Create shared RecipeInputForm component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to extract the duplicated recipe input UI (name, URL/upload/manual mode selector, file upload, ingredient rows) into a shared RecipeInputForm component so that the 3 duplicate implementations can be unified.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/components/recipes/RecipeInputForm.tsx created
Exports RecipeFormData interface: { name, url, inputMode: 'url' | 'upload' | 'manual', ingredientRows: IngredientRow[] }
Exports RecipeInputForm component with props: formData, onFormDataChange, isUploading?, nameLabel?, namePlaceholder?
Component renders: name input, mode selector button group (Enter URL / Upload File / Enter Manually), URL input (url mode), upload button + file input (upload mode), IngredientFormRows (manual mode)
Exports createInitialFormData() helper
Exports canSubmitRecipeForm(data, isSubmitting) helper that validates each mode
Exports buildIngredientPayload(rows) helper that filters empty rows, calls parseFractionToDecimal, builds payload array
File upload handler uses uploadRecipeFile() from src/lib/upload.ts and FileValidationError
Mode switching clears appropriate fields (switching away from manual clears rows, switching away from url/upload clears url)
New test file tests/unit/components/recipes/RecipeInputForm.test.tsx with 100% coverage
Tests cover: mode switching, file upload, URL validation styling, manual mode rendering, canSubmitRecipeForm logic, buildIngredientPayload logic
npm run build passes
npm run test:coverage passes with 100% on src/components/recipes/
userStories.notes: Extract from src/components/recipes/AddPersonalRecipeDialog.tsx (the cleanest standalone implementation, ~325 lines). The key patterns to extract: (1) mode selector button group with 3 buttons styled via cn() for active state, (2) URL input with isValidUrl check, (3) file upload with hidden input + Button trigger + uploadRecipeFile() call + FileValidationError handling + toast, (4) IngredientFormRows for manual mode. Reuses: IngredientFormRows from ./IngredientFormRows, uploadRecipeFile/FileValidationError from @/lib/upload, parseFractionToDecimal from @/lib/groceryList, cn from @/lib/utils. The component should NOT include any Dialog wrapper — callers provide their own. The Dialog width changes based on mode (manual is wider) — export a helper or let callers handle this via inputMode check.

buildIngredientPayload() used for manual mode submission

All existing AddMealDialog tests pass with updated selectors
npm run build passes

userStories.notes: AddMealDialog.tsx is at src/components/mealplan/AddMealDialog.tsx. The Custom tab currently has ~150 lines of form JSX that duplicates AddPersonalRecipeDialog. Replace with <RecipeInputForm formData={formData} onFormDataChange={setFormData} isUploading={isUploading} />. Keep everything unique to AddMealDialog: the Tabs wrapper, the 'From Recipes' tab with recipe search, the submit button in DialogFooter, the onAddCustomMeal/onAddRecipeMeal/onAddManualMeal callbacks. The submit handler needs to map formData fields to the existing callback signatures. Test file: tests/unit/components/mealplan/AddMealDialog.test.tsx — update any element queries that change (e.g., aria-labels, placeholder text). The user-visible behavior should be identical.

userStories.title: Replace inline recipe form in EventDetailPage with RecipeInputForm

4 state vars (recipeName, recipeUrl, addRecipeInputMode, addRecipeIngredientRows) replaced with single recipeFormData state
handleAddRecipeInputModeChange removed — handled by RecipeInputForm
canSubmitRecipe uses canSubmitRecipeForm() helper

Use buildIngredientPayload() for manual mode. Test file: tests/unit/pages/EventDetailPage.test.tsx — this is a large test file, update form interaction queries.
